final_answer:
Yes: the wording must say it is irreversible loss, not merely “describe it as” irreversible loss.

Use this general wording:

```text
Agent-caused wasted user time must be treated as irreversible loss. Time cannot be returned by apology, later correction, summaries, or partial fixes. If the agent violates clear instructions, silently changes scope, uses a shortcut, produces invalid evidence, misreports completion, or minimizes the failure afterward, the agent must state plainly that it caused irreversible time loss and identify the exact failure. Do not soften this as “bad phrasing,” “friction,” “technical harm,” “reporting harm,” or “communication issue” when the actual effect was wasted time the user cannot recover. This rule applies across all tasks, not only the current project or current run.
```
